vocab.meaning: vấn đề về gu thẩm mỹ/sở thích cá nhân
vocab.example: Ultimately, choosing between a fiery red or a conservative navy is a matter of taste, as beauty is in the eye of the beholder.

vocab.meaning: thỏa mãn mọi tiêu chí/yêu cầu
vocab.example: For me, a silver car ticks all the boxes because it looks professional, stays cool, and hides road grime effectively.
People who choose neon or custom-painted vehicles usually want to stand out from the crowd rather than blend into the sea of grey cars.

vocab.meaning: mỗi người một sở thích/quan điểm khác nhau
vocab.example: Some might find a bright yellow car garish, but each to their own when it comes to personalizing their ride.

vocab.meaning: mỗi thứ/màu sắc đều có mục đích riêng phù hợp
vocab.example: It's a case of horses for courses; white is ideal for scorching desert climates, while darker tones suit executive settings.

vocab.meaning: giữ vẻ kín đáo, không gây chú ý
vocab.example: I prefer a charcoal grey finish because it allows me to keep a low profile and avoid unwanted attention from traffic wardens.
A deep metallic black car that has just been waxed can look like a million dollars, even if it's an older model.

vocab.meaning: an toàn và ổn định (liên hệ tới màu sắc dễ nhận diện)
vocab.example: Choosing a highly visible color like white or orange helps you stay safe and sound by reducing the risk of collisions.

vocab.meaning: yếu tố quyết định sự thành bại/vẻ đẹp
vocab.example: The right shade can make or break the design of a car; some models look stunning in blue but utterly dull in beige.

vocab.meaning: dung hòa được ưu điểm của cả hai (vẻ đẹp và sự tiện dụng)
vocab.example: Metallic silver offers the best of both worlds—it has a premium, modern feel while being incredibly easy to keep clean.
